description: List files in long format with human-readable sizes, including hidden files.
Move up one directory level.
Print the current working directory path.
Create a directory and any necessary parent directories.
Create a new empty file or update the timestamp of an existing one.
Recursively and forcibly remove a directory and its contents (Use with caution!).
Copy a directory and its contents recursively.
Rename a file/directory or move it to a new location.
Search for a pattern recursively in the current directory.
Find all files matching a pattern in the current directory and subdirectories.
Display the entire contents of a file in the terminal.
Open a file for interactive reading (allows scrolling and searching).
Display the first 20 lines of a file.
Follow the end of a file in real-time (great for logs).
Make a script or file executable.
Change the owner and group of a file or directory.
Show all running processes on the system.
Display real-time system resources and active processes.
Forcibly terminate a process by its Process ID.
Show disk space usage for all mounted filesystems in human-readable format.
Show the total size of a specific folder.
Show available and used system memory (RAM) in MB.
Show all network interfaces and their IP addresses.
Check network connectivity to a host.
Download a file from a URL.
Another popular tool for downloading files from the web.
Connect to a remote machine via Secure Shell.
Securely copy a file to a remote machine.
Compress a folder into a .tar.gz archive.
Extract a .tar.gz archive.
Show detailed system and kernel information.
Display the username of the current active user.
Show a list of recently executed commands in the current shell.
Execute a command with superuser (root) privileges.
Display the manual page for a specific command.
Create a shortcut for a long command.
Display all active environment variables.
A simple, terminal-based text editor.
A powerful (but complex) terminal-based text editor.
